title: Methods of treatment for muscular disorders using small molecule modulation of alpha7 integrin
abstract: Disclosed herein are Î±7 integrin modulatory agents and methods of using such to treat muscular disorders, disease, degeneration, and/or muscular dystrophy. The methods include administering an effective amount of an Î±7 integrin modulatory agent to the subject with a muscular disorder, disease, degeneration, and/or dystrophy, wherein the Î±7 integrin modulatory agent increases Î±7 integrin expression or activity as compared to Î±7 integrin expression or activity prior to treatment, thereby treating the subject. Also disclosed are methods of enhancing muscle regeneration, repair, or maintenance in a subject and methods of enhancing Î±7 integrin expression by use of the disclosed Î±7 integrin modulatory agents. Methods of preventing or reducing muscle injury or damage in a subject are also disclosed.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1 . A method for treating a subject with a muscle disease or disorder, comprising: administering an effective amount of an a7 integrin modulatory agent to the subject with the muscle disease or disorder, wherein the a7 integrin modulatory agent comprises at least one of the following compounds: TRULI, TDI-011536, GA-017, VT02956, T65, and T74. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the muscle disease or disorder is muscle degeneration, comprising: administering an effective amount of an a7 integrin modulatory agent to the subject with the muscle degeneration, wherein the a7 integrin modulatory agent comprises at least one of the following compounds: TRULI, TDI-01 1536, GA-017, VT02956, T65, and T74. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein the muscle disease or disorder is muscular dystrophy, comprising: administering an effective amount of an a7 integrin modulatory agent to the subject with muscular dystrophy, wherein the a7 integrin modulatory agent comprises at least one of the following compounds: TRULI, TDI-01 1536, GA-017, VT02956, T65, and T74. 4. A method for treating a subject with a muscle disease or disorder, comprising: administering to the subject an effective amount of an a7 integrin modulatory agent, wherein the a7 integrin modulatory agent increases expression of a7 integrin, and wherein the a7 integrin modulatory agent comprises at least one of the following compounds: TRULI, TDI-011536, GA- 017, VT02956, T65, and T74. 5. The method of claim 2, wherein the muscle degeneration is a decrease in muscle strength of at least 20% relative to a control population. 6. The method of claim 2, wherein the muscle degeneration is a decrease in muscle mass of at least 20% in the subject relative to a measurement of muscle mass in the subject at a prior time point. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the prior time point is one year, two years, three years, four years, or five years earlier. 8. The method of claim 3, wherein the muscular dystrophy is selected from the group consisting of merosin deficient congenital muscular dystrophy Type 1A (MDC1 A), merosin deficient congenital muscular dystrophy Type 1 D (MDC1 D), limb-girdle muscular dystrophy (LGMD), Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD), Fukuyama congenital muscular dystrophy (FCMD), and Facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy (FSHD). 9. The method of claim 3, wherein the muscular dystrophy is Duchenne muscular dystrophy. 10. The method of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the a7 integrin modulatory agent is TRULL 11 . The method of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the a7 integrin modulatory agent is TDI-011536. 12. The method of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the a7 integrin modulatory agent is GA-017. 13. The method of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the a7 integrin modulatory agent is VT02956. 14. The method of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the a7 integrin modulatory agent is T65. 15. The method of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the a7 integrin modulatory agent is T74. 16. The method of any one of claims 1 to 15, further comprising administering to the subject an additional therapeutic agent. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the additional therapeutic agent is a protein, a growth factor satellite cells, stem cells, myocytes, an a7|31 integrin modulatory agent, or an additional a7 integrin modulatory agent. 18. The method of any one of claims 1 -17, wherein the subject is a human.
